title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Obfuscated Script Execution
description: Detects obfuscation techniques in command lines indicative of BLUELIGHT payload delivery. The RAT uses XOR encryption (key 0xCF), Base64 encoding, and SVG-embedded JavaScript for defense evasion during initial exploitation.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and (('Event ID' = 4688 and ('New Process Name' like '*\\powershell.exe' or 'New Process Name' like '*\\wscript.exe' or 'New Process Name' like '*\\cscript.exe' or 'New Process Name' like '*\\mshta.exe')) and (('Command Line' like '*FromBase64String*' or 'Command Line' like '*ToBase64String*' or 'Command Line' like '*-enc *' or 'Command Line' like '*-encodedcommand*' or 'Command Line' like '*XOR*' or 'Command Line' like '*char(*' or 'Command Line' like '*-bxor*' or 'Command Line' like '*iex(*' or 'Command Line' like '*Invoke-Expression*')))
falsepositives: Legitimate encoded PowerShell scripts used by IT automation
Software deployment tools using encoded parameters
